channel.title: AI Explained
channel.description: Daily bite-sized episodes explaining AI concepts — from quantization to transformers. Narrated by Boti.
channel.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Transformers — The architecture behind modern AI
item.description: How does the architecture powering GPT, Claude, and every major AI model actually work? We break down the transformer — from the limitations of older recurrent networks, to the self-attention mechanism that lets models process entire sentences at once. Queries, keys, values, multi-head attention, and why this 2017 paper changed everything.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Tokenization — How AI reads text
item.description: How do language models convert human text into numbers they can process? We explore tokenization — from simple character mapping to sophisticated subword algorithms like BPE. Why the word 'tokenization' might be split into multiple pieces, and why this invisible step shapes everything about AI text processing.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Quantization — How AI Models Shrink to Fit Your Laptop
item.description: What is quantization? How does it let you run massive AI models on a laptop with 8GB of RAM? We break down the math, the formats (GGUF, GPTQ, AWQ), and why neural networks are surprisingly tolerant of rounding.
item.itunes:author: Boti
